title: Xtra Citrusy Indian Buttermilk Chaat
description: A refreshing and energizing Indian dish featuring buttermilk, caramelized sugar, and citrus flavors, perfect for a hot day. This dish is also Buddhist-temple-friendly and can be served as a snack or dessert.
instructions: In a medium saucepan, combine the sugar, lemon juice, orange juice, and ginger juice, and heat over medium heat, stirring until the sugar dissolves.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to medium-low and simmer for 10 minutes, or until the mixture caramelizes and turns golden brown.
In a large bowl, whisk together the buttermilk, cumin powder, salt, and citric acid.
Add the caramelized sugar mixture to the buttermilk mixture and whisk until well combined.
In a small bowl, sprinkle the agar agar powder over 2 tablespoons of water and let it sit for 5 minutes to soften.
Add the softened agar agar to the buttermilk mixture and whisk until well combined.
Pour the mixture into individual serving cups or a large serving dish.
Refrigerate the mixture for at least 3 hours, or until it sets and becomes glassy-jelly like.
Serve the Xtra Citrusy Indian Buttermilk Chaat chilled, garnished with a sprinkle of cumin powder and a few slices of citrus fruit, if desired.
